evidence.scientific_clarity: All three numbered equations (ELBO, decomposition, retrieval-augmented variant) are rendered cleanly with proper LaTeX math, aligned environments, and cross-referenced prose; the derivation chain is legible and the scientific claim is fully conveyed.
evidence.typography: PDF produced by pdfTeX with all fonts embedded as Type 1 (CM family: CMR10, CMMI10, CMSY10, MSBM10, etc.), no DejaVu Sans, text is selectable/editable — no fonttype=42 concept applies here since this is a native LaTeX PDF, not matplotlib.
evidence.palette: Equation block is black-on-white with no color encoding; no colorblind accessibility issues possible; appropriate for a math derivation block.
evidence.layout_density: Single-page derivation block appears well-spaced with reasonable margins; PNG shows proper equation spacing; minor concern that blank space around the block could be tighter, but no overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption uses \eqref cross-references matching the labeled equations; caption accurately describes each equation's role; no legend needed; matches figure content precisely.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is a genuine pdfTeX-generated vector document (version 1.7), all 13 fonts embedded as subsetted Type 1, text is fully selectable; confirmed by pdffonts output.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf and figure.png present and non-trivial (138 KB PDF, 500 KB PNG); caption.tex and gen_figure.py present; however, equations.tex (required output per brief) is absent from the working directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption clearly identifies all three equations by number, describes their mathematical roles (Jensen's inequality, decomposition, retrieval conditioning), and includes specific mathematical claim that the model reduces to standard diffusion when R(x)=∅.

overall_strengths: Genuine pdfTeX vector output with fully embedded Type 1 Computer Modern fonts — professional LaTeX typography with selectable math throughout.
Caption precisely matches figure content, uses \eqref cross-references, and states a concrete mathematical reduction claim (R(x)=∅ recovers standard diffusion).
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex (explicitly required output per brief) is absent from the working directory — the generation script references it but it was cleaned up or never saved alongside the outputs.
Layout density is adequate but not maximally tight; some whitespace around the derivation block could be reduced for a journal-ready single-panel figure.
